meaning: vt.使显得愚笨,使变无效,使成为徒劳
examples: We must fight low expectations because they stultify ambition.
The regular use of calculators could stultify a child's capacity to do basic mental operations.
Things like machines may develop or neglect certain things in people... machines make our life impersonal and stultify certain elements in us and create an impersonal environment.
The level of supervision needed to root out all the mediocre will also stultify the brilliant.
The move towards it, in fact, if implemented too quickly, could stultify economic growth.
